description.gm:
When dislodged, mortal fragments like a chewed fingernail, a careless sneeze, or a few strands of hair might fall to the soil and be swiftly forgotten. When a horror like Charg brushes close to reality, the whispering remnants cast off from his nightmare form are not so harmless. At other times, the desecrated remains of innocent creatures can be transformed into whispering remnants as well. Worshippers of unholy gods and followers of evil philosophies gather these things and covet them as one might the hem of a cloak brushed against an idolized hero, or the visage of a divinity made manifest in a patch of mold on a wall. whispering remnants spread fear and cruelty wherever they're hidden and can corrupt those who discover them. They might be buried in the soil of a farmer's field, dropped into the depths of a village well, or stashed at the bottom of a toy box. Wherever the innocent might stumble across them and become unknowing actors in the decay of morality, the whispering remnant works its unholy magic.

A whispering remnant appears as a raw, decaying mass of flesh, fur, and gristle—a teratoma consisting of warty skin, sightless eyes, a finger or a toe, tufts of bristly hair, or teeth without mouths or lips to hide them. Each whispering remnant's shape is wildly different and uniquely unsettling. A whispering remnant automatically imparts to a PC the knowledge of how to use its Hear My Words activation as soon as it's touched, but its other activations must be discovered normally through the use of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Identify Magic] (and as normal, identifying the presence and effect of the curse it carries requires a critical success at such an attempt).

Unlike most cursed items, a character can discard a whispering remnant, yet doing so runs a real risk that it might thereafter fall into the wrong hands. In this Adventure Path, any whispering remnant the PCs discard anywhere other than the restored reliquary in the Maiserene Church at area A9 is discovered by one of the monsters of Bastardhall, who then Hides the horror somewhere in the region where it was found. Where the horror is hidden is left to you to decide.

As long as a creature carries a whispering remnant, its thoughts and dreams are influenced by cruel notions and temptations to commit violent depravities. A creature that's already predisposed toward these acts derives greater pleasure than before for such atrocities but receives no additional penalty or benefit, while a non-sapient creature is driven to become unnaturally violent in its actions (and over the course of weeks, becomes visibly corrupted by patches of strange fur, warty growths, unsightly teeth, or similar teratomas on its body). A sapient creature that isn't predisposed toward acts of depravity (likely including all PCs) takes a –9 item penalty to saving throws against all magical effects that attempt to encourage such acts. Saving throws against spells like @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Suggestion] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Charm] cast by creatures who attempt to push a PC into such acts, any affect by such creatures that attempts to control a PC, or certain specific effects mentioned in the text of this adventure would all be affected by this penalty. A creature with the holy trait reduces the overall item penalty imparted by this curse by 1, to a minimum penalty of 0.

A creature that's foolish enough to consume a whispering remnant becomes fused to its curse, at which point the whispering remnant remains lodged within the creature's body until the curse is removed from the creature with a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Cleanse Affliction] spell or similar magic, or upon the creature's death, whichever comes first. At this point, the whispering remnant crawls or oozes its way out of the creature's body and flops, once again inanimate, onto the ground, where it waits to be claimed by a new carrier.

As long as you carry a whispering remnant somewhere on your person, its curse does have a beneficial side: The power of its influence grants you a +3 item bonus to all saving throws against curses from other sources, as the whispering remnant jealously wards against such encroachments on its territory.

The whispering remnant periodically twitches and pulses as if it were alive.

Activate—Become As One 1 minute (concentrate, manipulate)

Effect By piling eight whispering remnants in a heap and then concentrating upon the mound of cursed flesh for a minute, you cause the eight whispering remnants to slither together and become a single whispering remnant. Once combined in this manner, the resulting whispering remnant cannot be thereafter split into individual whispering remnants.

Activate—Hear My Words 2 (concentrate, manipulate)

Frequency once per day

Effect The whispering remnant casts a DC 23 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Enthrall] spell to your specifications. Creatures that fail or critically fail to resist this spell perceive and remember the whispering remnant in your hand as something commonplace to the region, such as a farming tool, book, or fresh-baked pie, for as long as they remain enthralled. If you don't take advantage of this to subtly guide any affected creatures toward undermining their society, the whispering remnant can't be activated to Hear My Words again by you for 1 week. Additionally, the next time you sleep, you endure horrific nightmares and must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:30] save upon waking to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Stupefied]{Stupefied 1} for 24 hours (this is a mental effect).

Activate—Hide the Horror 10 minutes (manipulate)

Frequency once per year

Effect You hide the whispering remnant somewhere that, in time, it is likely to be found. For the following four weeks, the whispering remnant's evil seeps into the surrounding landscape, expanding its influence at a rate of 500 feet per day until it reaches its maximum emanation of 14,000 feet after 28 days. It continues to influence this area for another three weeks before going dormant—it must be recovered and rehidden later once this activation recharges to begin the cycle again. While the remnant's influence is active, any sapient creature that sleeps in the area experiences unsettling dreams of being stalked by monsters they can never quite see or evade. A creature that doesn't worship an unholy deity who experiences these dreams must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:30] save each time they rest to avoid awakening @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Fatigued]. On a critical failure, the creature awakens stupefied 1 as well; this condition remains until removed or until they are no longer fatigued, whichever comes first. Creatures who worship an unholy deity or follow a despicable philosophy are immune to this effect. Any skill checks made by such creatures when performing rituals performed in the affected area gain a +1 item bonus, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Nightmare] spells cast by them within the region have their save DCs increased by 1.

Activate—Hear My Whispers 2 (concentrate, manipulate)

Frequency once per day

Effect The whispering remnant casts a DC 30 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Subconscious Suggestion] spell to your specifications. If the target of the spell fails or critically fails to save against this, they perceive and remember the whispering remnant as being a commonplace item. If you don't suggest a course of action to the target that causes them to work at undermining their society, the whispering remnant shrieks horribly, but only in your mind—you take @Damage[10d6[mental]|traits:concentrate,manipulate] damage (@Check[will|dc:30|basic|traits:concentrate,manipulate] save), after which the whispering remnant cannot be activated again by you for 1 week.

Destruction A whispering remnant can't be destroyed, but a whispering remnant can be by hurling it into a powerful curse effect created by the deity associated with the remnant. This causes the whispering remnant to become reabsorbed into the deity's otherworldly body and often has potent side effects on the surrounding region. Disposing of a whispering remnant in the soul vortex of Bastardhall (area I) after Caydserris has been defeated not only destroys the whispering remnant but helps bring the Bastardhall Curse to an end.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies
